name: Class Recap — July 2
homework: Review Easy Japanese #35 (attached to the class email) and Lesson 5. Next class (7/9): share stories, watch Easy Japanese skit #37 (〜んですが to explain a problem and ask for help politely), and review verb conjugation.
topics_covered: が vs を with たい
それから vs そして
んです → のです (nominalizer の)
中 (なか) vs 内側 (うちがわ)

items: Form: u-verbs → い-form + たい; ru-verbs → drop る + たい. Add です to be polite (たいです).
The object of たい usually takes が (たまごが食べたい), though を is also possible.
何がしたい？ = What do you want to do? — a casual phrase among friends.

summary.h: Connecting verbs — て / それから / そして
items: The て-form joins two action verbs in one sentence: 大涌谷に行って、黒たまごが食べたいです。
それから = then / after that — a chronological sequence (order matters).
そして = and / also / furthermore — simple addition (order does not matter).

summary.h: んです / のです and the nominalizer の
items: In formal speech ん can become の: 黒いんですか → 黒いのですか. This の works like こと (a matter/fact).
黒いんですか？ adds the speaker's emotion — eager to confirm what they heard.
の also nominalizes an adjective: 黒いのが好きです = I like the black one. You cannot say 黒いは — add の.

summary.h: Inside & outside — 中 vs 内側
items: 外側 (そとがわ, outer side) ⇄ 内側 (うちがわ, inner side). 内側 is a surface/side relative to a boundary.
中 (なか) is the 3D space inside: 箱の中 = inside the box. 箱の内側 = the box's inner surface.
なか中は普通のゆで卵 = the inside is an ordinary boiled egg.

groups.name: 〜たい — Want To Do
groups.notes: い-form / drop る + たい(です). The object often takes が.

cards.back: verb stem + たい = want to [verb]
cards.notes: u-verbs → い-form + たい; ru-verbs → drop る + たい. たいです = polite.

cards.back: What do you want to do?
が is usual for the object of たい (を also OK)
cards.notes: たまごが食べたい / たまごを食べたい — both are possible.

cards.front: くろたまごがたべたいです
cards.frontSub: 黒たまごが食べたいです
cards.back: I want to eat a black egg
cards.notes: 食べる → 食べ + たいです

groups.name: Connecting Verbs — て / それから / そして
groups.notes: て joins two actions; それから orders them in time; そして simply adds.

cards.back: want to go and (then) eat — joined with て
cards.notes: て-form connects two action verbs: 大涌谷に行って、黒たまごが食べたいです。
Chronological sequence — actions happen in a specific order.
Parallel addition — connects facts/qualities equally, no time order.

groups.name: んです / のです & the Nominalizer の
groups.notes: In formal speech ん → の. の nominalizes (like こと) the word before it.

cards.back: ん becomes の in formal speech
cards.notes: 黒いんですか → 黒いのですか. This の = こと (it nominalizes).

cards.front: たまごはくろいんですか？
cards.backSub: tamago wa kuroi n desu ka?
cards.notes: んですか adds the speaker's eagerness to confirm what they heard.
の nominalizes 黒い (the black one). You cannot say 黒いは — add の.

groups.name: Inside & Outside — 中 vs 内側
groups.notes: 外側 ⇄ 内側 (a surface/side); 中 = the 3D space inside.

cards.notes: A surface/side relative to a boundary; implies an outer side (外側).
A 3D space inside: 箱の中 = inside the box.

cards.back: 中 = the space/volume inside; 内側 = the inner surface/side
cards.notes: 箱の中 = the items inside; 箱の内側 = the box's inner surface.
中は普通のゆで卵 = the inside is an ordinary boiled egg.
